description:
Goal: make Compiler2 lower `receive` as a first-class structured control construct that uses the existing runtime scheduler semantics and `dispatch_matrix`-owned pattern routing.

Signal:
- representative `receive` fixtures run through `fz2` on interp/JIT/AOT without reopening old-world receive lowering
- sender-side probing, pinned names, prepared dispatch keys, and after-clauses are carried by Compiler2-owned facts into backend/native consumers

Change:
- add Compiler2 lowering/model support for `receive do ... after ... end`
- use old-world receive code only as shape inspiration for invariants like pinned/capture threading and dispatch execution contracts
- preserve existing backend runtime intrinsics for send/spawn/self; only the missing structured receive surface is added

Why:
- backend runtime support already exists, but `fz2` still cannot compile the language construct that reaches it
title: Compiler2 selective receive: lower receive through dispatch_matrix and runtime facts
